channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: McDonald's earnings top estimates despite 'challenging environment'
item.description: Shares of McDonald's have fallen 10% over the last year, hurt by concerns about the broader economy.

item.title: Whirlpool says Iran war causing 'recession-level industry decline.' The shares are down 20%
item.description: "War in Iran resulted in recession-level industry decline in the U.S. as consumer confidence collapsed in late February and March," the company said.

item.title: Peloton beats estimates on revenue as higher subscription prices offer a boost
item.description: Peloton CEO Peter Stern told CNBC that the company believes raising prices on its subscriptions was a value-driven move.

item.title: Oil prices fall below $100 as U.S.-Iran tensions keep traders focused on Strait of Hormuz risks
item.description: Oil prices fell Thursday, as investors continue to assess the latest developments in the Middle East amid concerns over renewed tensions between Iran and the U.S.

item.title: This aluminum producer is on fire. Why Wells Fargo sees the stock rising even more
item.description: Analyst Timna Tanners upgraded the aluminum miner and smelter to overweight from equal weight.

item.title: Anthropic CEO says 80-fold growth in first quarter explains 'difficulties with compute'
item.description: At Anthropic's developer conference in San Francisco, CEO Dario Amodei said the company is 'working as quickly as possible' to provide additional compute.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: Whirlpool, Shake Shack, McDonald's, Arm Holdings & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in the premarket.

item.title: Goodbye quarterly earnings? Here's when traders believe this big change will happen
item.description: Traders are evenly divided as to whether the change will happen by Jan. 1. That would be an unusually fast timeline for the commission.

item.title: Family office deal-making rebounds in April with healthcare bets
item.description: Healthcare startups made up nearly a third of April's investments by family offices.

item.title: Shipping giant boss warns Iran war will have bigger impact in coming months as customers face price hikes
item.description: Maersk said the Iran war had introduced an "additional layer of uncertainty" as it reported first-quarter earnings on Thursday.

item.title: How the U.S. military and Maersk got a ship to pass through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Maersk CEO Vincent Clerc said the shipping giant had deliberately taken a "very cautious approach" during the Middle East crisis.

item.title: Gold and silver's historic rally could resume 'as fog of war lifts', market watchers say
item.description: On Thursday, spot gold jumped 1.2% to $4,750 per ounce amid hopes that the U.S. and Iran could be nearing a deal to bring the 69-day war to an end.

item.title: Amazon to carry Ozempic pill at U.S. kiosks, offer same-day delivery
item.description: Amazon said on Thursday its pharmacy will stock Novo Nordisk's Ozempic pill to treat type 2 diabetes at its kiosks and ⁠offer same-day delivery of the drug.

item.title: Elon Musk's Terafab chip factory in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, filing shows
item.description: Elon Musk's giant Terafab facility in Texas could cost up to $119 billion, with hopes of manufacturing chips for Tesla, SpaceX and xAI

item.title: Rare disease families find roadmap to drug development at bootcamps
item.description: Ultragenyx’s Rare Bootcamp gives families a roadmap to help drive research and develop new treatments for rare diseases.

item.title: Kraft Heinz CEO reveals the company’s biggest growth opportunities
item.description: Kraft Heinz CEO Steve Cahillane said the company's biggest growth opportunity is reinvesting behind its legacy brands.

item.title: Japan may have fired its yen bazooka twice, but markets are testing Tokyo’s resolve
item.description: Japan's Ministry of Finance has intervened in the yen over the country's Golden Week holiday.

item.title: Shell tops profit estimates as Iran war boosts oil price, cuts share buybacks
item.description: Britain's Shell posted stronger-than-expected quarterly profit as the Iran war sent fossil fuel prices soaring.

item.title: This oil tanker stock has outperformed. Bank of America sees more gains, even if U.S.-Iran war ends
item.description: The bank upgraded the oil transport name to buy from underperform.

item.title: EU weighs restricting use of U.S. cloud platforms to process sensitive government data, sources tell CNBC
item.description: There have been increasing calls within Europe for the region's most critical workloads to diversify away from dominant U.S. cloud providers.

item.title: Arm's quarter shows how it's carving a lucrative path in the crowded CPU resurgence
item.description: This central processing unit renaissance was confirmed when Intel reported earnings two weeks ago. AMD and now Arm told similar stories.

item.title: When should I refinance my mortgage? Plus, the best lenders to do it with.
item.description: When should I refinance my mortgage? The answer has to do with why a homebuyer wants to refinance.

item.title: It’s not just Big Oil. Wind giants welcome profit beats as Iran war spurs energy pivot
item.description: Norway's Equinor told CNBC that the company expects the Iran war to deliver a boost to its transition industries.

item.title: Iran war threatens jet fuel shortage that could disrupt summer travel in Asia and Europe
item.description: Oil executives have warned that the supply disruption in the Middle East will soon cause actual fuel shortages in some parts of the world.

item.title: Warner Bros. Discovery books $2.9 billion net loss tied to Paramount deal, restructuring costs
item.description: Paramount agreed to pay a Netflix termination fee, but the cost lives on WBD's books until the close of the deal.

item.title: DoorDash pops 12% on strong earnings, upbeat order growth guidance
item.description: DoorDash is in the middle of a massive spending initiative as it builds out a new tech platform following a string of acquisitions.

item.title: Snap issues cautious guidance as Perplexity deal ends, Middle East 'geopolitical situation' causes uncertainty
item.description: Snap reported first-quarter earnings and provided cautious sales guidance while revealing it no longer has a deal with the generative AI startup Perplexity.

item.title: Airlines spent 56.4% more on jet fuel in month after Iran war started, U.S. government says
item.description: U.S. airlines spent 56.4% more on jet fuel in March than they did in February, U.S. government data released Wednesday shows.

item.title: SoftBank shares surge over 18% as Japan tech-fueled rally lifts Nikkei 225 to record highs
item.description: The benchmark Nikkei 225 surged over 5% to a record high as Japan reopened after extended Golden Week holidays.

item.title: Will Elon Musk win his lawsuit against Sam Altman's OpenAI? Here's what prediction markets think
item.description: Trading activity on Elon Musk's suit against OpenAI is the second highest out of all prediction market contracts related to the billionaire.
